_doc: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for FanOps multi-agent lane orchestration. Edit THIS file to add/retire a lane or re-assign a hot file for a wave — nothing else. Consumers: (1) scripts/lane_guard.py — at pre-push + CI: resolves the branch's lane (explicit > `<lane>/` prefix > the branch's MOL id looked up in Linear via each lane's `linear` block) then FAILS a push/PR that edits a hot file owned by a DIFFERENT lane. (2) scripts/pr_collision_guard.py — in CI: FAILS a PR when a hot file it touches is ALSO being modified by another OPEN PR to main (the real merge-drift risk in the one-agent-per-ticket flow; needs no lane/Linear). (3) the fanops-orchestrator agent — ONLINE: uses each lane's `linear` block to pull the next READY ticket from Linear at runtime instead of a frozen list. Discovered Linear facts (2026-07-07): team 'Molham homsi'; statuses = Backlog | Todo | In Progress | In Review | Done | Canceled | Duplicate; every issue carries a canonical `gitBranchName`; active wave = project 'FanOps: CI Hardening (2026 Audit)' (MOL-181..199). Merge authority: the fanops-orchestrator lands PRs serially after sub-agent verification — a CONVENTION today, since the .cursor hook land-gate that once refused unverified merges is DORMANT (status owner: .orchestration/SPEC.md). Never enable code-owner-review branch protection — it would block the orchestrator's autonomous merge.

guard._doc: The guard is OPT-IN by branch prefix: a branch whose name does not start with a lane prefix below is IGNORED (exit 0). So normal branches (cursor/*, bycreamco/mol-*, human branches) are never blocked. Only files listed in `hot_files` are restricted; every other path is unrestricted. `owner` may be a single lane or a list (a genuinely shared hot file, coordinated in TIME by the orchestrator, not by this static guard).

linear.note: PROPOSED mapping (confirm/adjust). Set the label(s)/project that group publish-resilience work for the current wave.

picking.owns_note: Clip/moment/casting generation core (models, crosspost, ledger, casting, clip, moments, prompts).

linear.note: PROPOSED mapping (confirm/adjust). Set the generation-core label(s)/project for the current wave.

rfd.owns_note: Root-fix cross-cutting lane; shares moments.py/prompts.py with picking (TIME-coordinated by the orchestrator, never run concurrently on those files).

linear.note: PROPOSED mapping (confirm/adjust). Set the root-fix/robustness label(s)/project for the current wave.

ci.owns_note: CI / infra / tooling. No src/fanops hot files, so it is compatible with any source lane running in parallel.

linear.note: Matched by PROJECT (specific) — not the generic 'Improvement' label, which would over-match. The live wave as of 2026-07-07 (MOL-181..199).
